    <h1 {...{
      "id": "displaying-inbox"
    }}>{`Displaying Inbox`}</h1>
    <p>{`This tutorial explains how to fetch and display an Inbox in your application.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "pre-requisites"
    }}>{`Pre-requisites`}</h2>
    <p><a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/client-sdks/edge/adobe-journey-optimizer/#implement-extension-in-mobile-app"
      }}>{`Integrate and register AEPMessaging extension`}</a>{` in your app.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "overview"
    }}>{`Overview`}</h2>
    <p>{`The Inbox is a pre-built UI component that displays content cards in a unified container. Unlike individual content cards, the Inbox automatically manages loading states, error handling, empty states, and card layout based on server-side configuration from `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://business.adobe.com/products/journey-optimizer/adobe-journey-optimizer.html"
      }}>{`Adobe Journey Optimizer`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "get-inboxui"
    }}>{`Get InboxUI`}</h2>
    <p>{`To display an Inbox, call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`getInboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` with your configured surface. This API returns an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` object immediately, which manages its own state transitions automatically.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")
let inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: inboxSurface)
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`When created, the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` begins fetching inbox settings and content cards, transitioning through loading, loaded (with or without cards), or error states as needed.`}</p>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote">{`Note - The Inbox automatically handles layout (vertical/horizontal), styling, and unread indicators based on server-side configuration from Adobe Journey Optimizer campaigns.`}</p>
    </blockquote>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "display-inbox-in-swiftui"
    }}>{`Display Inbox in SwiftUI`}</h2>
    <p>{`The simplest way to display an Inbox in SwiftUI is to use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI.view`}</inlineCode>{` property:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import SwiftUI
import AEPMessaging

struct InboxPage: View {
    
    let inboxUI: InboxUI
    
    init() {
        let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")
        inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: inboxSurface)
    }
    
    var body: some View {
        NavigationView {
            inboxUI.view
                .navigationTitle("Inbox")
        }
        .onAppear {
            // Fetch content cards when the view appears
            let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")
            Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces([inboxSurface])
        }
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "display-inbox-in-uikit"
    }}>{`Display Inbox in UIKit`}</h2>
    <p>{`To display an Inbox in UIKit, use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`UIHostingController`}</inlineCode>{` to wrap the SwiftUI `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI.view`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`import UIKit
import SwiftUI
import AEPMessaging

class InboxViewController: UIViewController {
    
    var inboxUI: InboxUI!
    
    override func viewDidLoad() {
        super.viewDidLoad()
        
        title = "Inbox"
        
        // Create the InboxUI
        let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")
        inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: inboxSurface)
        
        // Wrap the SwiftUI view in a UIHostingController
        let hostingController = UIHostingController(rootView: inboxUI.view)
        
        // Add the hosting controller as a child view controller
        addChild(hostingController)
        view.addSubview(hostingController.view)
        hostingController.didMove(toParent: self)
        
        // Set up constraints to make the view fill the container
        hostingController.view.translatesAutoresizingMaskIntoConstraints = false
        NSLayoutConstraint.activate([
            hostingController.view.leadingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.leadingAnchor),
            hostingController.view.trailingAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.trailingAnchor),
            hostingController.view.topAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.topAnchor),
            hostingController.view.bottomAnchor.constraint(equalTo: view.bottomAnchor)
        ])
        
        // Fetch content cards
        Messaging.updatePropositionsForSurfaces([inboxSurface])
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "refreshing-inbox-data"
    }}>{`Refreshing Inbox Data`}</h2>
    <p>{`The Inbox provides multiple ways to refresh content cards:`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "1-pull-to-refresh-swiftui"
    }}>{`1. Pull-to-Refresh (SwiftUI)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Enable pull-to-refresh functionality to allow users to refresh content by pulling down on the Inbox:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`let inboxSurface = Surface(path: "inbox")
let inboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: inboxSurface)

// Enable pull-to-refresh
inboxUI.isPullToRefreshEnabled = true
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`When a user pulls to refresh:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`The Inbox calls `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` to fetch the latest content from the server`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`After the update completes, it calls `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`getPropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` to retrieve the updated content`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`The UI refreshes with the new content cards`}</li>
    </ol>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote">{`Note - Pull-to-refresh is disabled by default. Set `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`isPullToRefreshEnabled = true`}</inlineCode>{` to enable it.`}</p>
    </blockquote>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "2-programmatic-refresh"
    }}>{`2. Programmatic Refresh`}</h3>
    <p>{`You can programmatically refresh the Inbox using the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`refresh()`}</inlineCode>{` method:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`// Refresh the inbox programmatically
inboxUI.refresh()
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`This is useful for:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Refreshing on button taps`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Auto-refreshing at intervals`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Refreshing after specific app events`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "handling-refresh-events"
    }}>{`Handling Refresh Events`}</h3>
    <p>{`To be notified when a refresh completes (for both pull-to-refresh and programmatic refresh), use the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxEventListening`}</inlineCode>{` protocol:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`// Implement InboxEventListening to handle refresh completion
func onSuccess(_ inbox: InboxUI) {
    print("Inbox refreshed successfully")
}

func onError(_ inbox: InboxUI, _ error: Error) {
    print("Failed to refresh inbox: \\(error)")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`See `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "listening-inbox-events.md"
      }}>{`Listening to Inbox Events`}</a>{` for more details on event handling.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "best-practices"
    }}>{`Best Practices`}</h2>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Fetch Early`}</strong>{`: Call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`updatePropositionsForSurfaces`}</inlineCode>{` when your app launches or when the user navigates to the Inbox screen to ensure fresh content is available.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Surface Naming`}</strong>{`: Use descriptive surface paths that match your Adobe Journey Optimizer campaign configuration (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Surface(path: "inbox")`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Surface(path: "home_feed")`}</inlineCode>{`).`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Reuse InboxUI`}</strong>{`: Keep the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` instance alive as long as the Inbox view is visible. The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` maintains state and efficiently updates when content changes.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ol">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p">{`Handle Multiple Surfaces`}</strong>{`: If your app has multiple Inboxes (e.g., notifications, promotions), create separate `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`InboxUI`}</inlineCode>{` instances with different surfaces:`}</p>
      </li>
    </ol>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-swift"
      }}>{`// Notifications inbox
let notificationsInboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: Surface(path: "notifications"))

// Promotions inbox
let promotionsInboxUI = Messaging.getInboxUI(for: Surface(path: "promotions"))
`}</code></pre>
